name: Rhodinocichlidae
description: Rhodinocichlidae is a monotypic family of passerine bird found from Mexico to South America. The single species has long puzzled taxonomists due to its mix of thrush, tanager, and warbler characteristics.

children.name: Rhodinocichla
children.description: A monotypic genus of striking passerine with unclear taxonomic affinities, found in Central and South America.

children.name: Rhodinocichla rosea
children.description: The Rosy Thrush-tanager has rich rose-red underparts, dark grey upperparts, and a bold white supercilium. It skulks in dense undergrowth of lowland and foothill forests from Mexico to Venezuela and Colombia.
